sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the green box with W center is now inside the charcoal frame in the bottom-left quadrant. It appears to be inside the frame. Let me check if this counts as "placed" and if the level is complete.
Let me verify the containment and see what remains to be done.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Green boxes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
        for c in n['children']:
            cn = seg['nodes'][c]
            print(f"    child: color={cn['color']}")

print("\nContainment:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and 10 < n['pixels'] < 100:
        for child_id in n['children']:
            child = seg['nodes'][child_id]
            if child['color'] == 'N':
                print(f"  Green box id={child_id} inside charcoal frame id={n['id']}")

print("\nEmpty charcoal frames:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and 10 < n['pixels'] < 100:
        has_green = any(seg['nodes'][c]['color'] == 'N' for c in n['children'])
        if not has_green:
            print(f"  id={n['id']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
